entries.speaker: elara
lines:
My grandmother could call a storm lasting three days.
I called one last month that lasted three minutes. Progress.

entries.speaker: elara
lines:
The wind pact feels... thin. Like hearing someone
breathe in the next room. Present, but distant.

entries.speaker: elara
lines:
The pact broke. During the siege — I felt it snap,
like a bowstring. The spirit screamed once, then silence. I've
been calling every day since. Nothing.

entries.speaker: elara
lines:
The spirit isn't dead. I can feel it. It just won't
speak. Maybe it's afraid too.

entries.speaker: elara
lines:
She spoke. This morning. One word. Just my name.
I cried for an hour.
